comment.body: So, if I’m understanding this correctly, the bond market was about to blow up so Bessent and co let out some steam with the tariff announcement to avoid a blow-up, get vix down, and set off a short-squeeze. Yet at the same time, we now have 125% tariffs on China, 25% on Mexico/Canada, and possibly others? Maybe EU as well since they passed retaliatory tariffs, and 10% on everyone else? Seems like a good opportunity to get the fuck out of the market if one was caught in this drawdown. Would love to hear other thoughts/opinions and why I might be wrong in my assessment.

comment.body: 10y Auction was well received. 3y auction was from so bad dated back to March 2020. The Fed could be using back door access to fund today's auction without noticing...  still 10Y sitting 4.33 and real yields crossing the curve still up. That means market sees more inflation coming with rising term premiums along the entire curve.
